title: centrosema carajasense
intro: Centrosema carajasense, also known as the Carajas cowpea or tropical bean, is a legume plant native to the tropical regions of South America, particularly in the states of Pará and Maranhão in Brazil. It is a medicinal plant that has been used in traditional medicine due to its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing oxidative stress, improving cardiovascular health, and exhibiting potential antidiabetic and antihypertensive effects. Centrosema carajasense can be used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are often consumed as a tea or used topically for skin and wound care.
